subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, WHO, HRW, UNSW Kaldor Centre, Lowy, RSF) + international press; state media EXCLUDED. Extremely data-thin (~12,000 population); many factors are low-confidence inference. Uses the Australian dollar.

The world's smallest republic - a phosphate boom-and-bust left ~80% of the island mined-out and a squandered sovereign fund; now survives on rents from hosting Australia's offshore asylum/deportee processing plus fishing licenses.

Standout drags: near-total dependence on Australian rents (a new 30-year A$2.5bn deportee deal), documented harm to asylum seekers, a transactional 2024 China pivot + 'golden passport' scheme, and among the world's worst diabetes/obesity profiles.

economic.notes: An economy with essentially no productive base, surviving on Australian rents and using the Australian dollar.

fiscal_state.notes: Improved on the late-2024 Australia treaty plus an RPC revival - public debt ~15% of GDP, moderate distress risk, but utterly dependent on Australian rents. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Uses the Australian dollar - no monetary autonomy. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Elevated for the AUD bloc (~6%+ FY2025 moderating to ~4.5%) on import/supply factors. (IMF 2025)

banking_stability.notes: Historically had no commercial bank for years - thin, fragile financial access with a high SOE subsidy burden. (IMF; inference)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Effectively no productive export base post-phosphate - income is asylum-centre rent, fishing fees, passport sales, and aid. (IMF; Lowy)

institutional.notes: A history of judicial interference and opposition crackdowns, with no independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: A history of judicial interference, opposition crackdowns, and a magistrate/judge expulsion episode, with corruption concerns around the deportee deal. (Lowy; inference)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged but minimal-capacity, with opaque public-finance reporting. (IMF; inference)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A tiny administration heavily Australia/donor-supported, with large SOE distortions. (IMF; inference)

press_freedom.notes: RSF excludes Nauru as having no independent media; a history of restrictive measures (prohibitive media-visa fees, social-media bans). (RSF; inference)

political_social.notes: Historically volatile politics with a documented decade of harm to asylum seekers and a severe NCD crisis.

political_stability.notes: Historically volatile (frequent leadership turnover, past constitutional crises); President Adeang in office since Oct 2023, currently a stable patch. (Al Jazeera; inference)

civil_liberties.notes: Restrictions on press and protest historically; broadly tolerated personal life but weak political-expression space. (RSF; inference)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A decade-plus of documented harm/medical neglect of asylum seekers in the RPC; the new deportee deal strips procedural fairness. (HRW; UNSW Kaldor Centre; RNZ)

social_cohesion.notes: A small homogeneous Nauruan society, but an acute public-health crisis and a 'two-tier' dynamic around the detained/deportee population. (inference)

demographics.notes: Among the world's worst NCD profiles - diabetes ~21.8%, obesity >60%, life expectancy ~62 - a severe human-capital drag. (Global Obesity Observatory; WHO)

geopolitical.notes: Transactional - pivoted to China for aid while giving Australia an effective security veto.

alliance_reliability.notes: Transactional - pivoted to China (Jan 2024) for aid yet simultaneously gave Australia an effective veto over security/infrastructure deals. (Washington Post; Lowy)

conflict_involvement.notes: No conflict; a small contested piece in Pacific great-power competition. (inference)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions, but the golden-passport program and a past offshore-banking history create AML/integrity and reputational risk. (ANU; Henley; inference)

physical_practical.notes: A mined-out interior forcing coastal living, with an overwhelming NCD epidemic.

climate.notes: A raised-coral island (less SLR inundation) but a phosphate-mined, near-uninhabitable interior, water/heat stress, near-total import dependence. (IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Overwhelmed by an NCD epidemic (diabetes/CVD/renal) - ~75-80% of deaths from NCDs - with heavy overseas-referral reliance. (WHO; Global Nutrition Report)

infrastructure.notes: A single small island, donor/Australia-dependent, with degraded land and fragile power/water. (IMF; inference)

crime_safety.notes: A low baseline violent crime among Nauruans; the security flashpoints have centred on the detention population. (inference)

disaster_insurance.notes: Drought/climate exposure plus environmental degradation, with negligible insurance; relies on Australian transfers. (inference)

summary: The world's smallest republic (~12,000 people) and the archetypal resource-curse collapse - a phosphate boom left ~80% of the island mined-out and the sovereign fund squandered, leaving an economy with essentially no productive base. It now survives on rents from Australia: the offshore asylum-processing centre has supplied the majority of government revenue, and a new 30-year A$2.5bn deal (Aug 2025) to warehouse Australia's criminal-deportee 'NZYQ' cohort deepens that dependence, even as a decade of documented harm to asylum seekers weighs heavily. It pivoted from Taiwan to China in January 2024 for aid while granting Australia an effective security veto, and launched a world-first 'golden passport' scheme nominally funding climate relocation - carrying real integrity risk. With one of the planet's worst diabetes/obesity profiles and a life expectancy near 62, the skew is firmly negative, mitigated only by the Australia-backed fiscal lifeline and the recovering Intergenerational Trust Fund.
